title: The Senate Business and Professions Committee advanced legislation today by Senator Dave Cortese to address California’s severe nursing shortage, which has compromised patient care
prompt_preview:

Output JSON only matching the provided schema.

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California continues to suffer from a severe nursing workforce shortage.",
    "A staggering 92 out of 152 nursing programs report being denied access to clinical placements.",
    "The drop in the number of available nurses has caused delays in patient care.",
    "There is a harmful cycle of nurse attrition and burnout due to the nursing workforce shortage.",
    "SB 1015 aims to streamline the process for nursing students to secure clinical training.",
    "The bill helps ensure fair access to training opportunities for both private and public nursing programs.",
    "SB 1015 addresses the difficulty of nursing programs in securing enough clinical training placements.",
    "The bill seeks to create more opportunities for nursing students by setting equitable standards for clinical placements.",
    "SB 1015 empowers the Nursing Education and Workforce Advisory Committee to develop standards for nursing education and workforce advancement.",
    "The bill requires the BRN to gather and assess clinical placement availability and management data annually."
  ]
}
```
